practical_info.title: Practical information
practical_info.subtitle: Everything you need to know before exploring Algeria's heritage.
practical_info.disclaimer: Information accurate to the best of our knowledge. Always double-check with official sources (embassy, airline) before travelling.

visa.title: Visa and entry
visa.body: Entry requirements depend on the traveller’s nationality. Before departure, check the current rules with the Algerian consulate in your country of residence or through official authorities.

best_season.title: Best season by region
best_season.body:
🏖️ Mediterranean coast (Algiers, Oran, Annaba, Tipaza): May to October. Hot but bearable summers.

🏔️ Inland cities (Constantine, Tlemcen, Sétif): April–June, then September–October.

🏜️ Deep South (Hoggar, Tassili, Timimoun): October to April only. Temperatures can exceed 45°C in some Saharan areas in summer. Visits are strongly discouraged during the hottest hours.

transport.title: Getting around
transport.body:
Air Algérie connects all major cities (affordable domestic flights). The train is reliable between Algiers, Oran and Constantine.

For the Deep South, a car with driver or a local agency is strongly recommended. Renting a 4x4 is possible but a local guide adds a lot of value.

Taxis and ride-hailing (Yassir) are widely available in cities.

travel_times.title: Indicative travel times
travel_times.body:
Algiers → Tipaza: about 1 hour.

Constantine → Timgad: about 2 hours.

Sétif → Djemila: about 1 hour.

Tamanrasset → Assekrem: several hours with a guide.

Times vary with traffic, road conditions and local circumstances.

safety.title: Safety
safety.body:
Northern Algeria (coast, major cities, listed sites) is generally safe for tourists. Border areas with Mali, Niger and Libya remain not recommended.

Avoid flashing cash or camera gear in busy areas. Follow the travel advice from your own foreign ministry.

money.title: Currency and payments
money.body:
The currency is the Algerian dinar (DZD). It cannot be exported — exchange on arrival.

Official exchange is available through licensed institutions. Unofficial exchange practices may carry risks and are not recommended.

Foreign bank cards are rarely accepted outside major hotels. Cash is king.

ramadan.title: Travelling during Ramadan
ramadan.body:
During the holy month, daytime life slows down: restaurants closed by day, some sites with reduced hours. Nights, however, are lively and festive.

Out of respect, avoid eating, drinking or smoking in public during the day. Breaking the fast (Iftar) at sunset is a magical moment to share.

connectivity.title: Internet and phone
connectivity.body:
3 mobile operators: Djezzy, Mobilis, Ooredoo. Prepaid SIM cards available at the airport or in shops (passport required).

4G covers urban areas and main routes. Coverage in the Deep South is patchy — plan for offline.

etiquette.title: Customs and etiquette
etiquette.body:
Algerians are warm and hospitable. Mint tea is a welcome ritual — refusing may offend.

Modest dress is required in mosques and religious sites (shoulders and legs covered). Always ask before photographing people.

French is widely spoken, Arabic (fusha and darija) is the national language, Tamazight is also an official language.

prep.title: What to pack
prep.body:
• Passport photocopy
• Travel insurance
• Arabic/French translation app
• Power bank
• Water for excursions
• Suitable shoes for ancient sites

heritage.title: Protect the sites
heritage.body:
• Do not touch mosaics.
• Do not move stones or objects.
• Respect restricted areas and on-site instructions.
• Take your rubbish with you.

itineraries_page.title: Thematic itineraries
itineraries_page.subtitle: Ready-made journeys to discover Algeria differently — on the trail of the Romans, in the deep South, in Algiers…

timeline_page.title: The history of Algeria in dates
timeline_page.subtitle: From the first rock engravings to contemporary monuments — a complete timeline of Algerian heritage.

home.title: Algerian Heritage Guide
home.tagline: Roman sites, casbahs, oases and sacred places — discover their history in several languages.

footer.tagline: A collaborative guide to Algeria's cultural, historical and natural heritage, in several languages.
footer.about_text: Photos sourced from Wikimedia Commons, under open licenses (mostly CC BY-SA) — each photo's author is credited via the accompanying "Photo credit" link. Content is informational — check practical information (opening hours, prices) before your visit.
